pages.full_text:
blue ceiling of church

Saturday June 25th.
We walked up a zig zag path through fruit trees &
crawled out of a hole in the hedge into the road
circling the village. The church is beautiful & plain.
I lit a candle for Joan Grimley. Amazing views of
fields all round. A super [illegible] walk in an old
village with nice modern bits. Friendly people.
An old fort belonging to Medici's next to the church.
It would be good to come back. We bought bread.
Alex said that not only the Medici are
pages.notes: Photo shows interior of a church with blue vaulted ceiling. Caption 'blue ceiling of church' written in top-right corner of the page beside/above the photo. One word appears struck through before 'walk' — reads like a cancelled word, transcribed as [illegible]. 'Medici' and 'Medici's' likely refer to the Medici family; the fort/fort location and village are not named on this page. Year cannot be determined from this page alone; Saturday June 25 fell on e.g. 2005, 2011, 2016 within the volume range but no year is written.

pages.full_text:
from round here, but also the
della Casas.
pages.notes: Handwritten number '13' visible in top-right corner — may be a page number or annotation. Text is a continuation of the sentence from the left page: 'Alex said that not only the Medici are from round here, but also the della Casas.' 'della Casas' likely refers to the Casa/Casas family name (Italian). The rest of the page is blank.
